If Venom’s story continues in this current direction, I really hope they keep Knull around. That choice could take Venom into a darker, more nuanced place, giving the character layers beyond the usual “alien parasite in New York” setup. Eddie Brock has always been a bit lacking when it comes to motivation. Sure, Tom Hardy is fun to watch, but in these films, it never really feels like anything’s on the line. They’ve been entertaining, but mostly just mindless fun. 

The upcoming movie seems to be building on the pieces that worked, which is a good sign. That’s important because Venom’s character is deeply tied to Spider-Man. Venom only exists in contrast to Spider-Man—both Eddie and the symbiote fixate on him as a sort of ideal. Eddie’s jealousy of Peter Parker fuels his resentment, while the symbiote, having bonded with Spider-Man, has an even deeper connection to him. Spider-Man is at the core of Venom’s character, but it’s a one-sided obsession. Venom craves a connection with Spider-Man that he can’t have, which only fuels his rage.

Without Spider-Man, Venom just doesn’t work. He’s defined by his need to oppose and challenge him, like how Uncle Ben’s death defines Peter Parker. Venom is driven by this unattainable desire to take Spider-Man for himself, and when that fails, his obsession turns into destructive hatred. As long as Venom is deprived of that connection, he spirals into madness, thriving on resentment and aggression. But what makes Venom’s story really compelling is when Eddie rises above that. He learns to control the symbiote and use its power for good, transcending the role of Spider-Man’s adversary. His true journey is about breaking out of that cycle of bitterness and finding some sort of redemption or peace.

In his early days, Venom was nothing but pure chaos—a destructive force solely focused on taking down Spider-Man. He was the opposite of Spider-Man’s mantra, “With great power comes great responsibility.” Venom, instead, is driven by his own selfish needs and desires, using his power however he sees fit. This lack of connection only fuels his frustration, leading to dangerous, out-of-control behavior because it’s the only way he knows how to cope.

Sadly, the movies have dumbed down Venom’s character to a goofy alien story with some mediocre CGI fights and a weak romantic subplot. But this new trailer shows some promise. It looks like they’ve finally upped the stakes, and hopefully, this film will give Venom the depth and resolution the character’s been missing.

First venom movie: Eddie starts falling off a building because of a passing by airplane
Third venom movie: Venom casually doing aerobics on an airplane
Ya I hope they have an actual good reason for that instead of just pretending it didn’t happen.
@Mrllama. I guess that was the first time of Venom hearing that sound of the airplane. Now, he's probably used to. He might have evolved in that regard.

​@Mrllama.Could simply be Venom growing a tolerance towards more of the background sounds of human life. He was still new on Earth and with human hosts in the first movie, so that was likely the first airplane he ever heard. The sheer volume + his weakness to sonics made it unbearable. But by now he's probably heard dozens of planes flying around and other parts of human life that are loud af compared to the wilderness or the vacuum of space. So everyday sounds will bother him less and less, while he remains weak to more specific sonic frequencies.

But I probably put more thought into this than Sony ever put into the details of their SpiderlessVerse
@jahnybravo2168 That's a great point. Parasites mutate all the time so it makes sense. I hope they address it at least

The Venom Trilogy may not be a masterpiece; but they still are an amazing accomplishment !!!
Somehow, we got a trilogy about an anti-hero from one of the most lucrative character IPs of all time (Spider-Man) and the titular character Venom was not screwed up. He only improved after his disastrous film debut in Spider-Man 3, and is now one of Marvel's most popular and loved characters.

TBH, I enjoyed first 2 films and seems like everyone involved had fun; so, I am really looking forward to this and even more so now, as we have a Midnight Sons reference as well as a Knull storyline (wonder who is guest starring as him). Maybe even a Spider-Man cameo for a post-credits scene.

@blackhartxox  Friend, I am telling you that if Knull is killed by Venom in this movie, it would not change how the comics continue to show that that isn't necessarily possible. This is a movie, and it unfortunately can't spend 5 hours exploring just how deadly Knull is. Hell, they may even have Knull kill Venom/separate him from Eddie forever. The only way to find out is to watch the movie. The only way to be satisfied with these films is to know that they are loosely based on the canon established by the comics and, therefore, do not always adhere to what the fan's expectations of who the characters are. Knull could be a certified punk in this movie, and it would not ruin how he is portrayed in the Venom comics because these movies are not canon and bare no weight on their comic book portrayal. Just enjoy the movie as a Venum au of sorts and I think that maybe Knull's theoretical characterization won't bother you as much.
